 {"id":"bd-yluc","title":"Idea: rch config lint (risky settings warnings)","description":"## Background
Some config combinations are risky (e.g., excluding too much, disabling required patterns). A lint command can flag these early.

## Goals
- Add `rch config lint` to report potential misconfigurations.
- Provide actionable suggestions without blocking usage.

## Design / Approach
- Define lint rules: missing workers, excludes include target/, compression=0 with large projects, etc.
- Emit severity (warn/info) and remediation text.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Implement lint rule engine in config module.
- Add CLI subcommand + JSON output.
- Document lint rule list.

## Tests
- Unit: lint rules produce expected output.
- Integration: lint output includes severity and suggestion.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Lint surfaces common misconfigurations clearly.
- JSON output stable for CI usage.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-yluc.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Detect conflicting settings (force_local + force_remote).
- Warn on excludes that remove essential build dirs.
- Provide suppression mechanism for intentional configs.

## E2E Outline
- Create invalid config -> lint emits warnings with remediation.
- JSON output contains severity + suggestion.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/config.rs: lint rule engine and conflict detection.
- rch/src/commands.rs: lint output severity + remediation formatting.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-yluc.sh: invalid config triggers warnings; JSON output stable.

 {"id":"bd-zked","title":"Idea: Saved-time summary in rch status","description":"## Background
Users want a concrete sense of ROI. A simple saved-time summary increases perceived value and helps validate that offloading is worth it.

## Goals
- Show daily/weekly saved time and average speedup in `rch status --stats`.
- Use existing timing metrics with minimal overhead.

## Design / Approach
- Aggregate remote duration and estimated local duration per build.
- Compute saved_time = max(0, local_est - remote_time).
- Display totals and averages in human + JSON output.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add metrics aggregation for saved time.
- Extend status API and CLI display.
- Provide JSON fields for automation.

## Tests
- Unit: aggregation math + edge cases.
- Integration: status output includes saved time fields.
- E2E: mocked build history produces expected summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Status output shows saved time and speedup clearly.
- JSON output includes fields with stable names.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-zked.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Missing local estimates -> exclude from saved-time totals.
- Never report negative saved time.
- Define retention window for daily/weekly stats.

## E2E Outline
- Seed history with known timings -> totals match.
- JSON output includes saved_time fields.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch-common/src/types.rs: saved time aggregation and zero/negative handling.
- rchd/src/history.rs: windowed stats calculations.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-zked.sh: seed history, verify status --stats fields.

-{"id":"bd-zp4j","title":"Idea: Configurable remote temp base path","description":"## Background
Remote paths are currently fixed to /tmp/rch. Some environments require different locations (larger disks, noexec /tmp, sandboxed paths).

## Goals
- Allow configuring remote base directory for project sync and build execution.
- Maintain safe defaults and avoid path traversal risks.

## Design / Approach
- Add config: transfer.remote_base = "/tmp/rch" (default).
- Use remote_base in TransferPipeline remote_path computation.
- Ensure directory creation and cleanup respect new base.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add config parsing + validation (absolute path required).
- Wire remote_base into transfer pipeline and worker cleanup.
- Update docs and troubleshooting guidance.

## Tests
- Unit: remote path computation with custom base.
- Integration: rsync uses configured base path.
- E2E: custom base path works in mock mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can set custom remote base path safely.
- Existing default behavior unchanged if unset.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-zp4j.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- remote_base must be absolute and normalized; reject traversal.
- Tilde expansion allowed for user convenience.
- Cleanup/warm-cache use same base path.

## E2E Outline
- Set custom remote_base -> rsync uses new path.
- Invalid path -> config validation error.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs: remote_base normalization + absolute path validation.
- rch/src/config.rs: tilde expansion rules.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-zp4j.sh: set remote_base -> rsync uses new base; invalid path rejected.

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-09a","title":"Task: Integration Tests for Telemetry Pipeline","description":"## Overview
Implement integration tests for the complete telemetry pipeline: collection → aggregation → storage → API exposure.

## Background and Justification
The telemetry pipeline involves multiple components working together. Integration tests verify that data flows correctly through all stages without corruption or loss.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Collection to Storage Flow
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_telemetry_collection_to_storage() {
    // Setup
    let storage = TestStorage::new_in_memory();
    let collector = TelemetryCollector::new(storage.clone());
    
    // Collect telemetry
    collector.collect_once().await.unwrap();
    
    // Verify data in storage
    let stored = storage.get_latest_telemetry("test-worker").await.unwrap();
    assert!(stored.cpu_percent >= 0.0 && stored.cpu_percent <= 100.0);
    assert!(stored.memory_used_bytes > 0);
    assert!(stored.timestamp.elapsed() < Duration::from_secs(5));
}
```

### 2. Periodic Collection
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_periodic_collection() {
    let storage = TestStorage::new_in_memory();
    let collector = TelemetryCollector::new(storage.clone())
        .with_interval(Duration::from_millis(100));
    
    // Run for 500ms
    let handle = tokio::spawn(collector.run());
    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(550)).await;
    handle.abort();
    
    // Should have ~5 samples
    let samples = storage.count_samples().await;
    assert!(samples >= 4 && samples <= 6);
}
```

### 3. Storage to API Flow
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_storage_to_api() {
    // Setup
    let storage = TestStorage::new_in_memory();
    let api = TelemetryApi::new(storage.clone());
    
    // Insert test data
    storage.insert_telemetry(TelemetrySnapshot {
        worker_id: "test-worker".into(),
        cpu_percent: 42.5,
        memory_used_bytes: 1_000_000_000,
        timestamp: Utc::now(),
        ..Default::default()
    }).await.unwrap();
    
    // Query via API
    let response = api.get_worker_telemetry("test-worker").await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(response.cpu_percent, 42.5);
}
```

### 4. Aggregation Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_telemetry_aggregation() {
    let storage = TestStorage::new_in_memory();
    
    // Insert multiple samples
    for i in 0..10 {
        storage.insert_telemetry(TelemetrySnapshot {
            cpu_percent: 50.0 + i as f64,
            timestamp: Utc::now() - Duration::from_secs(i * 60),
            ..Default::default()
        }).await.unwrap();
    }
    
    // Query aggregated stats
    let stats = storage.get_aggregated_stats(
        "test-worker",
        Duration::from_secs(600),
        AggregationType::Average,
    ).await.unwrap();
    
    // Average of 50..59 = 54.5
    assert!((stats.cpu_percent - 54.5).abs() < 0.1);
}
```

### 5. Retention/Cleanup Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_telemetry_retention() {
    let storage = TestStorage::new_in_memory()
        .with_retention(Duration::from_secs(3600));  // 1 hour
    
    // Insert old and new data
    storage.insert_telemetry(TelemetrySnapshot {
        timestamp: Utc::now() - Duration::from_secs(7200),  // 2 hours old
        ..Default::default()
    }).await.unwrap();
    
    storage.insert_telemetry(TelemetrySnapshot {
        timestamp: Utc::now(),  // Current
        ..Default::default()
    }).await.unwrap();
    
    // Run cleanup
    storage.cleanup_expired().await.unwrap();
    
    // Should only have 1 sample
    assert_eq!(storage.count_samples().await, 1);
}
```

### 6. Multi-Worker Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_multi_worker_telemetry() {
    let storage = TestStorage::new_in_memory();
    
    // Insert for multiple workers
    for worker in ["css", "csd", "fmd", "yto"] {
        storage.insert_telemetry(TelemetrySnapshot {
            worker_id: worker.into(),
            cpu_percent: 50.0,
            ..Default::default()
        }).await.unwrap();
    }
    
    // Query all workers
    let all = storage.get_all_latest_telemetry().await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(all.len(), 4);
    assert!(all.iter().all(|t| t.cpu_percent == 50.0));
}
```

### 7. Error Handling Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_storage_failure_recovery() {
    let storage = TestStorage::new_failing();  // Simulates failures
    let collector = TelemetryCollector::new(storage);
    
    // Collection should not panic on storage failure
    let result = collector.collect_once().await;
    assert!(result.is_err());
    
    // Should continue operating
    let metrics = collector.get_error_metrics();
    assert_eq!(metrics.storage_failures, 1);
}
```

## Test Infrastructure

### Mock Storage
```rust
pub struct TestStorage {
    data: Arc<Mutex<Vec<TelemetrySnapshot>>>,
    should_fail: bool,
}

impl TelemetryStorage for TestStorage {
    async fn insert(&self, snapshot: TelemetrySnapshot) -> Result<()> {
        if self.should_fail {
            return Err(StorageError::ConnectionFailed);
        }
        self.data.lock().unwrap().push(snapshot);
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Test Worker
Create a mock worker that generates predictable telemetry:
```rust
pub struct MockWorker {
    cpu_pattern: Vec<f64>,  // CPU values to cycle through
    current_index: AtomicUsize,
}
```

## Dependencies
- Requires telemetry collection implementation
- Requires storage implementation
- Part of Testing epic

## Files to Create/Modify
- `rch-telemetry/tests/integration/pipeline.rs`
- `rch-telemetry/tests/integration/storage.rs`
- `rch-telemetry/tests/mocks/storage.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Collection → Storage flow tested
- [ ] Storage → API flow tested
- [ ] Aggregation logic verified
- [ ] Retention/cleanup works correctly
- [ ] Multi-worker scenarios covered
- [ ] Error handling tested","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:54:09.441060023Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T06:13:14.459422517Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T06:13:14.459422517Z","close_reason":"Implemented 20 integration tests covering: Collection to Storage flow, SpeedScore storage, multi-worker isolation, maintenance/aggregation, protocol serialization, and error handling","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-09a","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-a4q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-09a","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-q3u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-1eh","title":"Test Coverage Tooling: llvm-cov Integration","description":"## Overview
Set up code coverage measurement using llvm-cov or cargo-tarpaulin.

## Goals
- Measure line coverage across all crates
- Generate HTML reports for visualization
- Set coverage thresholds (target: 80%)
- Track coverage trends over time

## Implementation

### Install llvm-cov
```bash
cargo install cargo-llvm-cov
```

### Add to Makefile/justfile
```makefile
coverage:
	cargo llvm-cov --workspace --html
	@echo "Coverage report: target/llvm-cov/html/index.html"

coverage-check:
	cargo llvm-cov --workspace --fail-under-lines 80
```

### Coverage Exclusions
```toml
# .cargo/config.toml
[llvm-cov]
exclude = [
    "*/tests/*",
    "*/benches/*",
    "*/examples/*",
]
```

## Logging (for CI)
```
[coverage] Running: cargo llvm-cov --workspace
[coverage] Compiling with instrumentation...
[coverage] Running tests...
[coverage] Generating report...
[coverage] Overall line coverage: 78.5%
[coverage] By crate:
[coverage]   rch-common: 85.2%
[coverage]   rch: 72.1%
[coverage]   rchd: 81.3%
[coverage]   rch-wkr: 79.0%
[coverage] Report: target/llvm-cov/html/index.html
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] llvm-cov configured and working
- [ ] Coverage reports generate correctly
- [ ] Makefile/justfile commands added
- [ ] Coverage > 75% baseline established","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:34:22.504651506Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T22:58:35.056381275Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T22:58:35.056381275Z","close_reason":"Tooling added (Makefile + .cargo config); llvm-cov baseline 65.09% recorded; follow-up remote_compilation_helper-pacy to raise to >=75%","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-1f5","title":"Add shell completion generation (bash/zsh/fish)","description":"## Overview

Provide shell completion support for bash/zsh/fish in a way that is ergonomic for users across environments. This bead focuses on *installation and distribution* of completions (docs, setup/installer integration, idempotent install locations), and complements the dynamic completion engine in `remote_compilation_helper-77c`.

## Goals

1. Ensure completion scripts can be installed in common shell locations
2. Provide clear docs and setup guidance for enabling completions
3. Idempotent install (no duplicate entries in rc files)
4. Work in offline or restricted environments where dynamic completions are undesirable

## Scope

- Use `rch completions <shell>` output (from 77c) as the source
- Install to standard locations:
  - bash: `~/.bash_completion.d/` or `/etc/bash_completion.d/`
  - zsh: `~/.zfunc/` + `fpath`
  - fish: `~/.config/fish/completions/`
- Optionally integrate into `rch setup` or `install.sh --easy-mode`

## Tests

- Unit: completion generation does not error
- Integration: install script writes to target location and is idempotent
- E2E: `scripts/e2e_test.sh` installs completions in temp dirs, verifies files exist and rc modifications are not duplicated

## Logging

- E2E logs should include completion output size, install path, and whether rc file was modified

## Acceptance Criteria

- Completions can be installed with clear instructions
- Idempotent install verified across shells
- Works without requiring dynamic completion mode

## Dependencies

- Dynamic completions engine (remote_compilation_helper-77c)

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-1pm","title":"Rust Integration Tests: tests/ Directory Structure","description":"## Overview
Set up proper Rust integration test structure in tests/ directories.

## Directory Structure
```
rch/tests/
├── common/
│   ├── mod.rs           # Shared test utilities
│   ├── logging.rs       # Test logging setup
│   ├── fixtures.rs      # Test data fixtures
│   └── assertions.rs    # Custom assertions
├── integration/
│   ├── config_tests.rs  # Config loading integration
│   ├── command_tests.rs # CLI command integration
│   └── hook_tests.rs    # Hook integration
└── e2e/
    └── (linked to main e2e tests)

rchd/tests/
├── common/
│   └── mod.rs
├── integration/
│   ├── api_tests.rs     # API endpoint tests
│   └── worker_tests.rs  # Worker management tests
└── e2e/
    └── (linked to main e2e tests)
```

## Logging Setup (common/logging.rs)
```rust
use tracing_subscriber::{fmt, EnvFilter};

pub fn init_test_logging() {
    let _ = fmt()
        .with_test_writer()
        .with_env_filter(EnvFilter::from_default_env()
            .add_directive("rch=debug".parse().unwrap()))
        .try_init();
}

#[macro_export]
macro_rules! test_log {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        tracing::info!(target: "test", $($arg)*)
    };
}
```

## Fixture System (common/fixtures.rs)
```rust
pub struct TestProject {
    pub dir: TempDir,
    pub cargo_toml: PathBuf,
    pub src_main: PathBuf,
}

impl TestProject {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        test_log!("FIXTURE: Creating test Rust project");
        // Create minimal project
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/ directory structure created
- [ ] Shared utilities implemented
- [ ] Logging setup works
- [ ] Fixtures reusable across tests
- [ ] cargo test discovers all tests

-{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-1q7","title":"Epic: Web Dashboard SpeedScore Integration","description":"## Overview
Extend the existing RCH web dashboard (Next.js 16) to display worker SpeedScores, benchmark history, and performance visualizations, following patterns established in cloud_benchmarker.

## Background and Justification
The web dashboard currently shows worker status and basic metrics. Adding SpeedScore visualization provides:
- At-a-glance comparison of worker capabilities
- Historical trends showing performance changes
- Debugging tool for investigating slow compilations
- Confidence in worker selection decisions

cloud_benchmarker uses Plotly for interactive charts. We'll use a React-native charting solution for better Next.js integration (likely recharts or visx).

## Scope
This epic covers:
1. SpeedScore display cards for each worker
2. Benchmark history charts
3. Component score breakdown visualization
4. Real-time telemetry overlay
5. Benchmark trigger UI

## Subtask Breakdown
1. **API Endpoints**: Expose SpeedScore data via REST/WebSocket
2. **Worker Card Enhancement**: Add SpeedScore badge and trend indicator

4. **Benchmark History Chart**: Time-series of score changes
5. **Comparison View**: Side-by-side worker comparison
6. **Manual Benchmark Trigger**: Admin action to re-benchmark

## Design Principles
- Consistent with existing dashboard styling
- Mobile-responsive
- Accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- Real-time updates via WebSocket where applicable

## Dependencies
- Requires SpeedScore calculation engine
- Requires benchmark scheduler (for trigger functionality)
- Requires telemetry API endpoints

+{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-1q7","title":"Epic: Web Dashboard SpeedScore Integration","description":"## Overview
Extend the existing RCH web dashboard (Next.js 16) to display worker SpeedScores, benchmark history, and performance visualizations, following patterns established in cloud_benchmarker.

## Background and Justification
The web dashboard currently shows worker status and basic metrics. Adding SpeedScore visualization provides:
- At-a-glance comparison of worker capabilities
- Historical trends showing performance changes
- Debugging tool for investigating slow compilations
- Confidence in worker selection decisions

cloud_benchmarker uses Plotly for interactive charts. We'll use a React-native charting solution for better Next.js integration (likely recharts or visx).

## Scope
This epic covers:
1. SpeedScore display cards for each worker
2. Benchmark history charts
3. Component score breakdown visualization
4. Real-time telemetry overlay
5. Benchmark trigger UI

## Subtask Breakdown
1. **API Endpoints**: Expose SpeedScore data via REST/WebSocket
2. **Worker Card Enhancement**: Add SpeedScore badge and trend indicator
3. **SpeedScore Detail Panel**: Expandable component breakdown
4. **Benchmark History Chart**: Time-series of score changes
5. **Comparison View**: Side-by-side worker comparison
6. **Manual Benchmark Trigger**: Admin action to re-benchmark

## Design Principles
- Consistent with existing dashboard styling
- Mobile-responsive
- Accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- Real-time updates via WebSocket where applicable

## Dependencies
- Requires SpeedScore calculation engine
- Requires benchmark scheduler (for trigger functionality)
- Requires telemetry API endpoints

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-1t9","title":"Fix compilation errors blocking build","description":"## Critical Build Errors

The rch crate fails to compile due to API mismatches:

### Issues Identified:
1. **commands.rs uses ctx.style() but OutputContext has theme()** - Multiple locations call `ctx.style()` but the method doesn't exist
2. **ui/progress.rs uses ctx.theme().supports_unicode()** - Calling theme() method that exists but the API usage pattern may be inconsistent

### Root Cause:
API changes in OutputContext/Theme not propagated to all consumers.

### Fix Required:
- Replace `ctx.style()` with `ctx.theme()` in commands.rs
- Or add a `style()` method to OutputContext as an alias for theme()

### Files Affected:
- rch/src/commands.rs
- rch/src/ui/progress.rs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T05:32:13.034034289Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T05:33:56.457408053Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T05:33:56.457408053Z","close_reason":"Fixed by adding style() method alias to OutputContext at rch/src/ui/context.rs:370-375. Build now succeeds with only warnings.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-20k","title":"Add terminal hyperlinks (OSC 8) for clickable URLs","description":"## Overview

Add terminal hyperlinks using OSC 8 for clickable URLs in supported terminals. Provide safe fallbacks for non‑TTY and opt‑out control.

## Goals

1. OSC‑8 links when supported
2. Fallback to plain text URLs
3. Config/env toggle (RCH_LINKS=0)
4. Avoid OSC‑8 in JSON/plain modes

## Implementation

- Detect TTY + TERM support
- Wrap URLs as `\x1b]8;;URL\x1b\\TEXT\x1b]8;;\x1b\\`
- Provide `link(text, url)` helper in UI module

## Tests

- Unit: OSC‑8 formatting
- Unit: fallback in non‑TTY
- Integration: ensure no OSC‑8 when `--json` or `RCH_LINKS=0`
- E2E: `scripts/e2e_test.sh` runs a command that emits help links and logs whether OSC‑8 was emitted in TTY vs non‑TTY modes

## Logging

- E2E logs should explicitly show link rendering decisions

## Acceptance Criteria

- Links clickable in supported terminals
- No OSC‑8 in logs or JSON output

## Dependencies

- Output abstraction layer (remote_compilation_helper-u0v)

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-266","title":"E2E Tests: Fleet Deployment Operations","description":"## Overview
E2E tests for fleet deployment operations.

## Test Cases

### 1. test_fleet_deploy_new_version
**Scenario**: Deploy new RCH version to all workers
**Steps**:
1. Start with 3 test workers
2. Run `rch fleet deploy --all`
3. Verify all workers updated
**Expected Logging**:
```
[e2e::fleet] TEST: test_fleet_deploy_new_version
[e2e::fleet] SETUP: 3 workers configured
[e2e::fleet] DEPLOY: Starting deployment of v0.5.0
[e2e::fleet] DEPLOY: worker-1: transferring binary (2.1 MB)
[e2e::fleet] DEPLOY: worker-2: transferring binary (2.1 MB)
[e2e::fleet] DEPLOY: worker-3: transferring binary (2.1 MB)
[e2e::fleet] VERIFY: worker-1: version=0.5.0 ✓
[e2e::fleet] VERIFY: worker-2: version=0.5.0 ✓
[e2e::fleet] VERIFY: worker-3: version=0.5.0 ✓
[e2e::fleet] PASS: test_fleet_deploy_new_version
```

### 2. test_fleet_deploy_rollback
**Scenario**: Deployment fails, rollback triggered
**Expected**: Previous version restored on all workers

### 3. test_fleet_health_monitoring
**Scenario**: Monitor fleet health over time
**Expected**: Health status updated, alerts triggered

### 4. test_fleet_partial_deploy
**Scenario**: Deploy to subset of workers
**Expected**: Only specified workers updated

### 5. test_fleet_concurrent_operations
**Scenario**: Multiple fleet operations in parallel
**Expected**: No conflicts, all complete

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Full deployment tested
- [ ] Rollback tested
- [ ] Partial deployment tested
- [ ] Logs show all operations

+/// JSON structure for --help-json output.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, schemars::JsonSchema)]
+struct HelpJsonOutput {
+    name: String,
+    version: String,
+    about: Option<String>,
+    subcommands: Vec<SubcommandHelp>,
+    global_flags: Vec<ArgHelp>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, schemars::JsonSchema)]
+struct SubcommandHelp {
+    name: String,
+    about: Option<String>,
+    aliases: Vec<String>,
+    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    subcommands: Vec<SubcommandHelp>,
+    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    arguments: Vec<ArgHelp>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, schemars::JsonSchema)]
+struct ArgHelp {
+    name: String,
+    short: Option<char>,
+    long: Option<String>,
+    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    help: Option<String>,
+    required: bool,
+    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    default_value: Option<String>,
+    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
+    possible_values: Vec<String>,
+}
+
+/// Handle --help-json flag: output CLI structure as JSON for machine parsing.
+fn handle_help_json(command: &Option<Commands>) -> Result<()> {
+    let cmd = Cli::command();
+
+    let output = match command {
+        None => {
+            // Full CLI structure
+            build_help_json(&cmd)
+        }
+        Some(subcmd) => {
+            // Find the specific subcommand
+            let subcmd_name = get_command_name(subcmd);
+            if let Some(sub) = cmd.get_subcommands().find(|s| s.get_name() == subcmd_name) {
+                build_help_json(sub)
+            } else {
+                eprintln!("Unknown subcommand: {subcmd_name}");
+                std::process::exit(1);
+            }
+        }
+    };
+
+    let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&output)?;
+    println!("{json}");
+    Ok(())
+}
+
+fn get_command_name(cmd: &Commands) -> &'static str {
+    match cmd {
+        Commands::Init { .. } => "init",
+        Commands::Daemon { .. } => "daemon",
+        Commands::Workers { .. } => "workers",
+        Commands::Status { .. } => "status",
+        Commands::Queue { .. } => "queue",
+        Commands::Cancel { .. } => "cancel",
+        Commands::Config { .. } => "config",
+        Commands::Diagnose { .. } => "diagnose",
+        Commands::Hook { .. } => "hook",
+        Commands::Agents { .. } => "agents",
+        Commands::Completions { .. } => "completions",
+        Commands::Doctor { .. } => "doctor",
+        Commands::SelfTest { .. } => "self-test",
+        Commands::Update { .. } => "update",
+        Commands::Fleet { .. } => "fleet",
+        Commands::SpeedScore { .. } => "speedscore",
+        Commands::Dashboard { .. } => "dashboard",
+        Commands::Web { .. } => "web",
+    }
+}
+
+fn build_help_json(cmd: &clap::Command) -> HelpJsonOutput {
+    HelpJsonOutput {
+        name: cmd.get_name().to_string(),
+        version: cmd.get_version().map(|v| v.to_string()).unwrap_or_default(),
+        about: cmd.get_about().map(|a| a.to_string()),
+        subcommands: cmd
+            .get_subcommands()
+            .filter(|s| !s.is_hide_set())
+            .map(build_subcommand_help)
+            .collect(),
+        global_flags: cmd
+            .get_arguments()
+            .filter(|a| a.is_global_set() && a.get_id() != "help" && a.get_id() != "version")
+            .map(build_arg_help)
+            .collect(),
+    }
+}
+
+fn build_subcommand_help(cmd: &clap::Command) -> SubcommandHelp {
+    SubcommandHelp {
+        name: cmd.get_name().to_string(),
+        about: cmd.get_about().map(|a| a.to_string()),
+        aliases: cmd.get_all_aliases().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect(),
+        subcommands: cmd
+            .get_subcommands()
+            .filter(|s| !s.is_hide_set())
+            .map(build_subcommand_help)
+            .collect(),
+        arguments: cmd
+            .get_arguments()
+            .filter(|a| a.get_id() != "help" && a.get_id() != "version")
+            .map(build_arg_help)
+            .collect(),
+    }
+}
+
+fn build_arg_help(arg: &clap::Arg) -> ArgHelp {
+    ArgHelp {
+        name: arg.get_id().to_string(),
+        short: arg.get_short(),
+        long: arg.get_long().map(|s| s.to_string()),
+        help: arg.get_help().map(|h| h.to_string()),
+        required: arg.is_required_set(),
+        default_value: arg
+            .get_default_values()
+            .first()
+            .map(|v| v.to_string_lossy().to_string()),
+        possible_values: arg
+            .get_possible_values()
+            .iter()
+            .map(|v| v.get_name().to_string())
+            .collect(),
+    }
+}
+
+/// JSON structure for --capabilities output.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, schemars::JsonSchema)]
+struct CapabilitiesOutput {
+    /// RCH version
+    version: String,
+    /// Build timestamp if available
+    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
+    build_timestamp: Option<String>,
+    /// Supported compilation runtimes
+    runtimes: Vec<RuntimeCapability>,
+    /// Available CLI commands
+    commands: Vec<CommandCapability>,
+    /// Feature flags and their status
+    features: Vec<FeatureCapability>,
+    /// Supported hook formats
+    hook_formats: Vec<String>,
+    /// Machine-readable output formats
+    output_formats: Vec<String>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, schemars::JsonSchema)]
+struct RuntimeCapability {
+    name: String,
+    description: String,
+    /// File extensions associated with this runtime
+    extensions: Vec<String>,
+    /// Example commands that trigger this runtime
+    example_commands: Vec<String>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, schemars::JsonSchema)]
+struct CommandCapability {
+    name: String,
+    description: String,
+    /// Brief category: "setup", "monitoring", "management", "configuration"
+    category: String,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, schemars::JsonSchema)]
+struct FeatureCapability {
+    name: String,
+    description: String,
+    enabled: bool,
+}
+

+
+    let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&output)?;
+    println!("{json}");
+    Ok(())
+}
